title: AppointmentRequest
description: NET-NEW write object: a request to book an appointment at NYC Health + Hospitals. This is the single most common patient transaction and it has NO machine-readable contract today — patients book by calling 1-844-NYC-4NYC or through a MyChart flow, and the live Epic FHIR R4 surface exposes NO Appointment create, NO Slot resource, and NO $book/$find scheduling operation. So even though H+H already runs a real FHIR standard, booking falls outside it. This schema is the request payload; a successful request yields an Appointment (see appointment.json). It intentionally models a 'request' (proposed) rather than a confirmed booking, matching how safety-net scheduling actually works (triage, callback, insurance/coverage check).

x-fhir.note: would map to Appointment.status=proposed / an Appointment $book request, neither exposed on the H+H FHIR endpoint today

requestId.description: Server-assigned request identifier. Omit on create; returned on the created resource.

status.description: Request lifecycle. Set by H+H, not the requester.

service.description: The service line / specialty the appointment is for (e.g. 'Primary Care', 'Cardiology', 'Behavioral Health').

facility.description: Preferred facility. Optional — H+H may triage to the nearest facility offering the service.

newPatient.description: Whether the requester is a new patient to H+H.

preferredDates.description: Preferred date windows, in priority order.

urgent.description: Flag time-sensitive (but non-emergency) requests. True emergencies must call 911 or go to an Emergency Department, not book here.

patient.description: Requester details. For an authenticated MyChart patient these may be inferred from the token.

mrn.description: H+H Medical Record Number, if known.

coverage.description: Coverage / financial context, so intake can route to NYC Care or financial counseling when there is no insurance.

notes.description: Additional notes or accommodation needs (interpreter, accessibility).

resultingAppointmentId.description: If scheduled, the id of the resulting Appointment.
